docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Bhuvanamitra S

Geospatial Data Analyst | Team Lead

Turning GeoData to Geo-enabled Insights

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
Geospatial Manager and Remote Sensing Specialist with 6+ years of hands-on experience delivering end-to-end GIS solutions, satellite data analytics, and spatial intelligence products across agriculture, environmental monitoring, and aviation domains. Proven ability to build and lead cross-functional teams, manage full project lifecycles using Agile methodologies, and translate complex spatial data into actionable client deliverables. Experienced in QGIS, Python assisted automation, and multi-spectral satellite analysis. Adept at stakeholder coordination, sprint planning, delivery tracking, and documentation using Jira and Confluence. Track record of driving GIS product development, quality assurance, and on-time delivery across multidisciplinary programs.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• GIS & Remote Sensing

    • QGIS, Google Earth Engine
    • GDAL / OGR, GRASS GIS
    • Multispectral and High Resolution Analysis

  • Programming (AI Assisted)

    • Python — GeoPandas, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2
    • JavaScript — Leaflet, MapLibre GL
    • SQL, PostgreSQL + PostGIS

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

  • GIS for Aviation

    • Obstacle Analysis and eTOD as per ICAO Standards
    • Aerodrome Mapping Database
    • Flight Procedure Design Tool development

Download CV

docs.title: Geospatial Manager — SatSure Analytics India Pvt. Ltd.
docs.text:
January 2023 – Present | Bengaluru, India

  • Led end-to-end geospatial product development across agriculture intelligence, environmental monitoring, and aviation data domains, managing cross-functional teams using Agile/Scrum methodologies with Jira and Confluence.
  • Spearheaded crop classification workflows using multi-spectral, multi-temporal satellite data for agri-finance and agri-insurance clients across diverse agro-climatic zones.
  • Led development of GIS-based aviation data products including Obstacle Limitation Surfaces (OLS), Aerodrome Mapping Databases (AMDB), and Electronic Charting modules compliant with ICAO Annexes 4, 14 & 15, deployed across Indian and international airports.
  • Developed full-stack geospatial data solutions in SatSure SKIES for transmission line route optimisation using high-resolution satellite imagery.
  • Authored and published GeoBearing-Distance, an open-source QGIS plugin for ellipsoidal bearing-distance geometry generation, available on GitHub and the QGIS Plugin Repository.

docs.title: Team Lead, Remote Sensing Operations — SatSure Analytics India Pvt. Ltd.
docs.text:
November 2021 – December 2022 | Bengaluru, India

  • Led remote sensing operations team across multiple agriculture and environmental projects, managing workload allocation, QA reviews, and client delivery timelines.
  • Supervised LULC mapping and multi-spectral crop classification using time-series satellite data across diverse geographies, enforcing quality thresholds and metadata standards.
  • Conducted internal knowledge-transfer sessions training junior analysts on GIS tools, satellite data processing, and delivery workflows.

docs.title: Data Analyst, GIS — SatSure Analytics India Pvt. Ltd.
docs.text:
January 2021 – October 2021 | Bengaluru, India

  • Performed advanced geospatial analysis for environmental impact assessment and LULC projects, producing client maps, spatial statistics, and analytical reports.
  • Built geospatial visualisations and dashboards using QGIS and Tableau integrated with satellite-derived datasets.

docs.title: Junior Data Analyst, GIS — SatSure Analytics India Pvt. Ltd.
docs.text:
January 2020 – December 2020 | Bengaluru, India

  • Led LULC classification project around Desert National Park, Rajasthan — conducted stratified ground-truth surveys and achieved 85% classification accuracy.
  • Produced detailed spatial maps and statistical analysis reports for client and stakeholder submission.

docs.title: Project Trainee, GIS — SatSure Analytics India Pvt. Ltd.
docs.text:
September 2019 – December 2019 | Bengaluru, India

  • Performed unsupervised crop classification using time-series multi-spectral satellite data and built storyboards for crop studies.
  • Evaluated and validated CHIRPS open-source satellite rainfall products against ground station records; the recommended product was adopted in production, replacing a commercial data source and saving ₹25 Lakhs annually.

docs.title: M.Tech. (Research) in Remote Sensing & GIS
docs.text:
National Institute of Technology Karnataka (NITK), Surathkal | 2019

Thesis: Evaluating the Potential of CHIRPS Rainfall Data in Streamflow Forecasting Using Soft-Computing Techniques — published in Water Resources Management (DOI: 10.1007/s11269-019-02340-6). CGPA: 7.92.

docs.title: B.Engg. in Civil Engineering
docs.text:
Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology & Management, Udupi | 2017

Score: 71.3% | Active member of ISTE, NSS-YRC, and Rotaract Club.

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
